target.profile_backing: After pass44 introduced dense F64 LiteralBuffer storage, scalar-tensor F64 broadcast still iterated Literal values instead of the existing dense contiguous F64 kernel.
target.baseline_command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-scalar-baseline rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- scalar

one_lever.name: dense F64 scalar broadcast route
one_lever.description: F64 scalar-tensor and tensor-scalar Add/Sub/Mul/Div now use LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice plus fj_lax::dense::scalar_op when the tensor is already dense-backed. Literal-backed and malformed declared-F64 tensors continue through the previous per-Literal fallback path.

after.command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-scalar-rebench rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- scalar

delta.comparison_row: eval/lt_scalar_1k_f64_vec unchanged within noise and not routed through this arithmetic fast path

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Dense scalar broadcast maps the tensor's dense F64 slice in the same row-major order as the previous Literal loop and collects one output per input element in that order.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: No comparisons, sorting, dispatch priority, or tie-breaking policy changed. The fast path only handles Add/Sub/Mul/Div.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Each lane uses the same scalar IEEE-754 f64 operation as the previous float_op closure. There is no reduction, reassociation, fused multiply-add, approximate math, unsafe code, C BLAS, LAPACK, MKL, XLA, or cross-lane reordering.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No random state, seed, sampling order, or nondeterministic iteration path changed.
isomorphism_proof.operand_order: scalar_on_left is forwarded into dense::scalar_op, preserving Sub and Div operand order for Scalar x Tensor and Tensor x Scalar separately.
isomorphism_proof.malformed_tensors: The dense route is only entered when LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice is present. Declared-F64 tensors constructed with malformed Literal payloads keep the previous per-Literal fallback behavior and remain literal-backed.

validation.command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-tests-remote rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax f64_scalar_broadcast_fast_path -- --nocapture
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-scalar-rebench rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- scalar
validation.result: pass on vmi1293453; scalar_mul 1219.4 ns -> 299.66 ns, tensor_sub_scalar 1130.5 ns -> 361.29 ns

validation.command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-clippy rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass46-tests rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax f64_scalar_broadcast_fast_path -- --nocapture
validation.result: local fallback due active-project exclusion; not counted as proof

notes: This is the next dense-storage lever under frankenjax-mcqr.30 after pass44. It is structural, not another rejected residual same-shape F64 Add micro-tune.
No C BLAS, MKL, LAPACK, XLA, unsafe code, stash, reset, clean, file deletion, or behavior-changing floating-point rewrite was used.
